question_patterns: what should i eat for breakfast
intro: Here are some breakfast ideas that fit the vault well.
why_it_fits: Breakfast answers should suggest a small number of realistic options instead of listing everything.
Use breakfast recipes that feel repeatable and practical.
caution: A few realistic breakfast options are more useful than listing the whole vault at once.

question_patterns: what should i eat for dinner
intro: Here are some dinner ideas that stay practical for the evening.
why_it_fits: Dinner answers should stay practical and not overwhelm the user.
Use a small number of realistic options from the dinner recipes.
caution: A few realistic dinner options are more useful than listing the whole vault at once.

intro: These are some of the stronger high-protein options in your vault.
why_it_fits: High-protein answers should favor meals with stronger protein support rather than only carb-heavy options.
Use protein-rich recipes the user could actually repeat.
caution: Protein support works best inside a balanced meal with fiber-rich carbohydrates and vegetables, not as a single macro target.
follow_up_suggestions: Ask for a light high-protein meal.

question_patterns: give dinner examples with high protein
give me dinner examples with high protein
intro: These dinner picks have the strongest protein numbers in the vault.
why_it_fits: High-protein dinner answers should show the protein number so the user can verify why the recipe was chosen.
Use dinner recipes with listed nutrition instead of relying on the title alone.
caution: Protein numbers are per serving from the recipe vault, so portions and ingredient changes can shift the final amount.
follow_up_suggestions: Ask for a lighter high-protein dinner.
Ask for high-protein meals for PCOS.

question_patterns: what should i eat if i want something light
intro: Here are some lighter-feeling options from the vault.
why_it_fits: Lighter meal answers should prefer gentler, easier-feeling dishes rather than just the lowest calorie number.
Soft grain dishes, soups, kanji, and stews often fit this better.
caution: A lighter meal should still be filling enough to be useful; avoid making it so small that it turns into an unsatisfying snack.
follow_up_suggestions: Ask for a light meal with more protein.

intro: These breakfast options give you better protein support.
why_it_fits: Breakfast should support satiety and steadier energy, not just fast carbs.
Use breakfast recipes with stronger protein support.
caution: The strongest breakfast picks combine protein support with slower-digesting carbohydrates rather than relying only on a high protein number.

question_patterns: what should i eat for a light dinner
intro: These dinner options are a better fit when you want something lighter.
why_it_fits: Lighter dinners should feel easier in the evening without becoming unrealistic.
Soups, kanji, stews, and softer grain dishes often work well.
caution: A light dinner should still feel like a real meal, with enough protein, fiber, or volume to keep it practical.

question_patterns: what meals are good for pcos
intro: These are practical meal directions that fit a PCOS-friendly approach.
why_it_fits: PCOS-focused meals should emphasize balanced meals, higher-fiber carbohydrates, and protein support.
The best follow-up is a few realistic meal suggestions rather than one perfect PCOS diet.
caution: One meal does not fix PCOS by itself; the useful goal is a repeatable pattern with fiber, protein support, and less added sugar.

question_patterns: what meals are good for triglycerides
intro: These are practical meal directions that fit a triglyceride-friendly approach.
why_it_fits: Triglyceride-friendly meals should emphasize vegetables, legumes, whole grains, leaner patterns, and less added sugar.
A few practical food examples work better than a long meal list.
caution: Food quality and consistency matter more than one magic ingredient; added sugar, refined carbohydrates, and alcohol are the bigger triglyceride-focused levers.
follow_up_suggestions: Ask for ingredient swaps for high triglycerides.

question_patterns: what should i eat before gym
intro: These are practical pre-workout meal directions from the vault.
why_it_fits: Pre-workout meals should be familiar, easy to digest, and not too heavy.
Soft grain dishes and moderate portions work better than very oily or very large meals.
caution: Keep the portion practical and allow digestion time before training.
follow_up_suggestions: Ask for a beginner gym day.

question_patterns: what should i eat after gym
intro: These are practical post-workout meal directions from the vault.
why_it_fits: Post-workout meals should support recovery with protein, fiber-rich carbohydrates, and enough fluid.
Dal, legumes, curd-based sides, and balanced grain bowls fit better than a sugar-heavy snack.
caution: A post-workout meal does not need to be extreme; a normal balanced meal is usually enough.
follow_up_suggestions: Ask for a simple day of eating.

intro: These breakfast options are better when you want something filling.
why_it_fits: Filling breakfasts should combine protein support, fiber, and slower-digesting carbohydrates.
Dal, legumes, oats, ragi, and whole-grain styles usually work better than a very refined breakfast.
caution: If appetite is low, choose a smaller portion rather than skipping protein support entirely.

intro: These are better directions when you want more fiber in the meal.
why_it_fits: Fiber-focused meals should lean on legumes, vegetables, greens, and whole grains or millets.
This also fits many PCOS and triglyceride-friendly meal patterns.
caution: Increase fiber gradually if the user is not used to it, and keep fluids in the day.

intro: These meal directions use dal, beans, or legumes for stronger staying power.
why_it_fits: Legume-based meals give protein support, fiber, and better satiety than a grain-only plate.
They are useful for both PCOS-friendly and triglyceride-friendly patterns when cooked practically.
caution: Choose familiar portions if legumes cause bloating, and build up gradually.
follow_up_suggestions: Ask for a simple day of eating.

intro: These are better directions when you want to avoid coconut.
why_it_fits: No-coconut answers should prefer dal, tomato, tamarind, greens, or vegetable-led dishes over coconut-heavy stews and chutneys.
If a recipe depends on coconut for identity, a different recipe is usually cleaner than a forced swap.
caution: Check the recipe ingredients before promising coconut-free, because coconut can appear in chutneys, stews, and kurma-style dishes.

intro: These are better directions when the goal is less added sugar.
why_it_fits: Low-added-sugar meals should rely on vegetables, legumes, whole grains, millets, and savory flavors.
This fits high-triglyceride and PCOS-friendly meal patterns better than sweetened foods.
caution: Focus on repeatable savory meals rather than treating one low-sugar meal as the whole solution.
follow_up_suggestions: Ask what to eat for PCOS.

question_patterns: what can i eat when not hungry
intro: These are better directions when appetite is low but you still want something useful.
why_it_fits: Low-appetite meals should be gentle, warm, and easy to finish.
Soup, rasam, kanji, kichadi, idli, and softer stews fit this better than heavy fried meals.
caution: If low appetite is persistent, severe, or connected with illness symptoms, it should not be treated as only a meal-planning issue.
